DESCRIPTOR.LABEL: GSC MIxS Hydrocarbon
DESCRIPTOR.NAME: GSC MIxS Hydrocarbon
DESCRIPTOR.DESCRIPTION: Genomic Standards Consortium package extension for reporting of measurements and observations obtained from the environment where the sample was obtained. By choosing the environmental package, a selection of fields can be made from a relevant subsets of the GSC terms.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Marine Sample

FIELD.LABEL: sample true vertical depth subsea
FIELD.NAME: sample_true_vertical_depth_subsea
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Depth of the sample i.e. The vertical distance between the sea level and the sampled position in the subsurface. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples e.g. 1325.75-1362.25 m

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics: ecosystem

FIELD.LABEL: trophic level
FIELD.NAME: trophic_level
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Trophic levels are the feeding position in a food chain. Microbes can be a range of producers (e.g. chemolithotroph)

FIELD.LABEL: observed biotic relationship
FIELD.NAME: observed_biotic_relationship
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of relationship(s) between the subject organism and other organism(s) it is associated with. E.g., parasite on species X; mutualist with species Y. The target organism is the subject of the relationship, and the other organism(s) is the object.

FIELD.LABEL: known pathogenicity
FIELD.NAME: known_pathogenicity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: To what is the entity pathogenic, for instance plant, fungi, bacteria

FIELD.LABEL: relationship to oxygen
FIELD.NAME: relationship_to_oxygen
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Is this organism an aerobe, anaerobe? Please note that aerobic and anaerobic are valid descriptors for microbial environments

FIELD.LABEL: propagation
FIELD.NAME: propagation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The type of reproduction from the parent stock. Values for this field is specific to different taxa. For phage or virus: lytic/lysogenic/temperate/obligately lytic. For plasmids: incompatibility group. For eukaryotes: sexual/asexual. Mandatory for MIGs of eukayotes, plasmids and viruses.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: methods, storage and transport

FIELD.LABEL: sample transport conditions
FIELD.NAME: sample_transport_conditions
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Sample transport duration (in days or hrs) and temperature the sample was exposed to (e.g. 5.5 days; 20 °C)

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection device
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_device
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The device used to collect an environmental sample. It is recommended to use terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO) and/or terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094).

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection method
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method employed for collecting the sample. Can be provided in the form of a PMID, DOI, url or text.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage temperature
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_temperature
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: temperature at which sample was stored, e.g. -80

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage location
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_location
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room. Indicate the location name.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: local environment conditions: soil

FIELD.LABEL: source rock depositional environment
FIELD.NAME: source_rock_depositional_environment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Source rock depositional environment (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Source_rock). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: site related

FIELD.LABEL: sample measured depth
FIELD.NAME: sample_measured_depth
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: In non deviated well, measured depth is equal to the true vertical depth, TVD (TVD=TVDSS plus the reference or datum it refers to). In deviated wells, the MD is the length of trajectory of the borehole measured from the same reference or datum. Common datums used are ground level (GL), drilling rig floor (DF), rotary table (RT), kelly bushing (KB) and mean sea level (MSL). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: corrosion rate at sample location
FIELD.NAME: corrosion_rate_at_sample_location
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Metal corrosion rate is the speed of metal deterioration due to environmental conditions. As environmental conditions change corrosion rates change accordingly. Therefore, long term corrosion rates are generally more informative than short term rates and for that reason they are preferred during reporting. In the case of suspected MIC, corrosion rate measurements at the time of sampling might provide insights into the involvement of certain microbial community members in MIC as well as potential microbial interplays

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection point
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_point
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Sampling point on the asset were sample was collected (e.g. Wellhead, storage tank, separator, etc). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: depth (tvdss) of hydrocarbon resource temperature
FIELD.NAME: depth_tvdss_of_hydrocarbon_resource_temperature
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: True vertical depth subsea (TVDSS) of the hydrocarbon resource where the original temperature was measured (e.g. 1345 m).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: core sample properties

FIELD.LABEL: oxygenation status of sample
FIELD.NAME: oxygenation_status_of_sample
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: oxygenation status of sample

FIELD.LABEL: density
FIELD.NAME: density
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: density of sample

FIELD.LABEL: sample well name
FIELD.NAME: sample_well_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the well (e.g. BXA1123) where sample was taken

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: non-sample terms: study or project

FIELD.LABEL: project name
FIELD.NAME: project_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: non-sample terms

FIELD.LABEL: ploidy
FIELD.NAME: ploidy
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The ploidy level of the genome (e.g. allopolyploid, haploid, diploid, triploid, tetraploid). It has implications for the downstream study of duplicated gene and regions of the genomes (and perhaps for difficulties in assembly). For terms, please select terms listed under class ploidy (PATO:001374) of Phenotypic Quality Ontology (PATO), and for a browser of PATO (v 2018-03-27) please refer to http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/PATO

FIELD.LABEL: number of replicons
FIELD.NAME: number_of_replicons
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reports the number of replicons in a nuclear genome of eukaryotes, in the genome of a bacterium or archaea or the number of segments in a segmented virus. Always applied to the haploid chromosome count of a eukaryote. Mandatory for MIGS of eukaryotes, bacteria, archaea and segmented virus.

FIELD.LABEL: extrachromosomal elements
FIELD.NAME: extrachromosomal_elements
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Do plasmids exist of significant phenotypic consequence (e.g. ones that determine virulence or antibiotic resistance). Megaplasmids? Other plasmids (borrelia has 15+ plasmids).

FIELD.LABEL: estimated size
FIELD.NAME: estimated_size
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The estimated size of the genome (in bp) prior to sequencing. Of particular importance in the sequencing of (eukaryotic) genome which could remain in draft form for a long or unspecified period. Mandatory for MIGS of eukaryotes.

FIELD.LABEL: target gene
FIELD.NAME: target_gene
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Targeted gene or locus name for marker gene studies

FIELD.LABEL: target subfragment
FIELD.NAME: target_subfragment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of subfragment of a gene or locus. Important to e.g. identify special regions on marker genes like V6 on 16S rRNA

FIELD.LABEL: multiplex identifiers
FIELD.NAME: multiplex_identifiers
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Molecular barcodes, called Multiplex Identifiers (MIDs), that are used to specifically tag unique samples in a sequencing run. Sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

FIELD.LABEL: sequence quality check
FIELD.NAME: sequence_quality_check
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Indicate if the sequence has been called by automatic systems (none) or undergone a manual editing procedure (e.g. by inspecting the raw data or chromatograms). Applied only for sequences that are not submitted to SRA or DRA

FIELD.LABEL: chimera check software
FIELD.NAME: chimera_check_software
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Tool(s) used for chimera checking, including version number and parameters, to discover and remove chimeric sequences. A chimeric sequence is comprised of two or more phylogenetically distinct parent sequences.

FIELD.LABEL: relevant electronic resources
FIELD.NAME: relevant_electronic_resources
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A related resource that is referenced, cited, or otherwise associated to the sequence in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

FIELD.LABEL: relevant standard operating procedures
FIELD.NAME: relevant_standard_operating_procedures
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

FIELD.LABEL: 16s recovered
FIELD.NAME: _16s_recovered
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Can a 16S gene be recovered from the submitted bin, SAG or MAG?

FIELD.LABEL: well identification number
FIELD.NAME: well_identification_number
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier of a well or wellbore. This is part of the Global Framework for Well Identification initiative which is compiled by the Professional Petroleum Data Management Association (PPDM) in an effort to improve well identification systems. (Supporting information: https://ppdm.org/ and http://dl.ppdm.org/dl/690)

FIELD.LABEL: water cut
FIELD.NAME: water_cut
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Current amount of water (%) in a produced fluid stream; or the average of the combined streams

FIELD.LABEL: production rate
FIELD.NAME: production_rate
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Oil and/or gas production rates per well (e.g. 524 m3 / day)

FIELD.LABEL: field name
FIELD.NAME: field_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the hydrocarbon field (e.g. Albacora)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Collection event information

FIELD.LABEL: collection date
FIELD.NAME: collection_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[12][0-9]{3}(-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?(/[0-9]{4}(-[0-9]{2}(-[0-9]{2}(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?)?$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: altitude
FIELD.NAME: altitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The altitude of the sample is the vertical distance between Earth's surface above Sea Level and the sampled position in the air.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ((0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (latitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_latitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (longitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_longitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (region and locality)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_region_and_locality
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.

FIELD.LABEL: broad-scale environmental context
FIELD.NAME: broadscale_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: local environmental context
FIELD.NAME: local_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: environmental medium
FIELD.NAME: environmental_medium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).

FIELD.LABEL: elevation
FIELD.NAME: elevation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The elevation of the sampling site as measured by the vertical distance from mean sea level.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ([+-]?(0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD.LABEL: secondary and tertiary recovery methods and start date
FIELD.NAME: secondary_and_tertiary_recovery_methods_and_start_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Additional (i.e. Secondary, tertiary, etc.) recovery methods deployed for increase of hydrocarbon recovery from resource and start date for each one of them. If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection

FIELD.LABEL: amount or size of sample collected
FIELD.NAME: amount_or_size_of_sample_collected
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected.

FIELD.LABEL: organism count
FIELD.NAME: organism_count
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Total cell count of any organism (or group of organisms) per gram, volume or area of sample, should include name of organism followed by count. The method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) Should also be provided. (example: total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qpcr)

FIELD.LABEL: organism count qpcr information
FIELD.NAME: organism_count_qpcr_information
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: If qpcr was used for the cell count, the target gene name, the primer sequence and the cycling conditions should also be provided. (Example: 16S rrna; FWD:ACGTAGCTATGACGT REV:GTGCTAGTCGAGTAC; initial denaturation:90C_5min; denaturation:90C_2min; annealing:52C_30 sec; elongation:72C_30 sec; 90 C for 1 min; final elongation:72C_5min; 30 cycles)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: unusual properties

FIELD.LABEL: hydrocarbon type produced
FIELD.NAME: hydrocarbon_type_produced
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Main hydrocarbon type produced from resource (i.e. Oil, gas, condensate, etc). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: api gravity
FIELD.NAME: api_gravity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: API gravity is a measure of how heavy or light a petroleum liquid is compared to water (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API_gravity) (e.g. 31.1API)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage duration
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_duration
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Duration for which the sample was stored. Indicate the duration for which the sample was stored written in ISO 8601 format.

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (country and/or sea)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_country_andor_sea
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of where the sample was collected from, with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).

TEXT_VALUE.VALUE: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: host disorder

FIELD.LABEL: host disease status
FIELD.NAME: host_disease_status
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: list of diseases with which the host has been diagnosed; can include multiple diagnoses. The value of the field depends on host; for humans the terms should be chosen from DO (Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, other hosts are free text

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: host description

FIELD.LABEL: host scientific name
FIELD.NAME: host_scientific_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Scientific name of the natural (as opposed to laboratory) host to the organism from which sample was obtained.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: local environment conditions

FIELD.LABEL: pressure
FIELD.NAME: pressure
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: pressure to which the sample is subject, in atmospheres

FIELD.LABEL: temperature
FIELD.NAME: temperature
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: temperature of the sample at time of sampling

FIELD.LABEL: pH
FIELD.NAME: ph
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: pH measurement

FIELD.LABEL: viscosity
FIELD.NAME: viscosity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A measure of oil's resistance to gradual deformation by shear stress or tensile stress (e.g. 3.5 cp; 100 C)

FIELD.LABEL: permeability
FIELD.NAME: permeability
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Measure of the ability of a hydrocarbon resource to allow fluids to pass through it. (Additional information: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Permeability_(earth_sciences))

FIELD.LABEL: oil water contact depth
FIELD.NAME: oil_water_contact_depth
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Depth of the original oil water contact (OWC) zone (average) (m TVDSS)

FIELD.LABEL: hydrocarbon resource original pressure
FIELD.NAME: hydrocarbon_resource_original_pressure
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Original pressure of the hydrocarbon resource

FIELD.LABEL: source rock geological age
FIELD.NAME: source_rock_geological_age
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Geological age of source rock (Additional info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Period_(geology)). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: concentration measurement

FIELD.LABEL: ammonium
FIELD.NAME: ammonium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of ammonium

FIELD.LABEL: sample salinity
FIELD.NAME: sample_salinity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Salinity is the total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid (in the form of an extract obtained by centrifugation) sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater

FIELD.LABEL: calcium
FIELD.NAME: calcium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of calcium

FIELD.LABEL: chloride
FIELD.NAME: chloride
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of chloride

FIELD.LABEL: suspended solids
FIELD.NAME: suspended_solids
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of substances including a wide variety of material, such as silt, decaying plant and animal matter, etc,; can include multiple substances

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved carbon dioxide
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_carbon_dioxide
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved inorganic carbon
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_inorganic_carbon
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: dissolved inorganic carbon concentration

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved inorganic phosphorus
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_inorganic_phosphorus
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of dissolved inorganic phosphorus

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved organic carbon
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_organic_carbon
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of dissolved organic carbon

FIELD.LABEL: magnesium
FIELD.NAME: magnesium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of magnesium

FIELD.LABEL: nitrate
FIELD.NAME: nitrate
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of nitrate

FIELD.LABEL: nitrite
FIELD.NAME: nitrite
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of nitrite

FIELD.LABEL: total nitrogen concentration
FIELD.NAME: total_nitrogen_concentration
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of nitrogen (total). Total nitrogen concentration of water samples, calculated by: total nitrogen = total dissolved nitrogen + particulate nitrogen. Can also be measured without filtering, reported as nitrogen

FIELD.LABEL: potassium
FIELD.NAME: potassium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of potassium

FIELD.LABEL: salinity
FIELD.NAME: salinity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater.

FIELD.LABEL: sodium
FIELD.NAME: sodium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: sodium concentration

FIELD.LABEL: sulfate
FIELD.NAME: sulfate
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of sulfate

FIELD.LABEL: sulfide
FIELD.NAME: sulfide
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: concentration of sulfide

FIELD.LABEL: total phosphorus
FIELD.NAME: total_phosphorus
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: total phosphorus concentration, calculated by: total phosphorus = total dissolved phosphorus + particulate phosphorus. Can also be measured without filtering, reported as phosphorus

FIELD.LABEL: total iron
FIELD.NAME: total_iron
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of total iron in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: volatile fatty acids
FIELD.NAME: volatile_fatty_acids
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of Volatile Fatty Acids in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved iron
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_iron
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of dissolved iron in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: resins wt%
FIELD.NAME: resins_wt
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene(SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

FIELD.LABEL: dissolved oxygen in fluids
FIELD.NAME: dissolved_oxygen_in_fluids
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of dissolved oxygen in the oil field produced fluids as it contributes to oxgyen-corrosion and microbial activity (e.g. Mic).

FIELD.LABEL: total sulfur
FIELD.NAME: total_sulfur
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of total sulfur in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: injection water fraction
FIELD.NAME: injection_water_fraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Proportion of the produced fluids derived from injected water at the time of sampling. (e.g. 87%)

FIELD.LABEL: ethylbenzene
FIELD.NAME: ethylbenzene
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of ethylbenzene in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: vfa in formation water
FIELD.NAME: vfa_in_formation_water
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Original volatile fatty acid concentration in the hydrocarbon resource

FIELD.LABEL: total acid number
FIELD.NAME: total_acid_number
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Total Acid Number(TAN) is a measurement of acidity that is determined by the amount of potassium hydroxide in milligrams that is needed to neutralize the acids in one gram of oil. It is an important quality measurement of crude oil. (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Total_acid_number)

FIELD.LABEL: sulfate in formation water
FIELD.NAME: sulfate_in_formation_water
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Original sulfate concentration in the hydrocarbon resource

FIELD.LABEL: toluene
FIELD.NAME: toluene
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of toluene in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: asphaltenes wt%
FIELD.NAME: asphaltenes_wt
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene(SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

FIELD.LABEL: xylene
FIELD.NAME: xylene
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of xylene in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: alkalinity method
FIELD.NAME: alkalinity_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method used for alkalinity measurement

FIELD.LABEL: benzene
FIELD.NAME: benzene
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Concentration of benzene in the sample

FIELD.LABEL: alkalinity
FIELD.NAME: alkalinity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: alkalinity, the ability of a solution to neutralize acids to the equivalence point of carbonate or bicarbonate

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Pointer to physical material

FIELD.LABEL: source material identifiers
FIELD.NAME: source_material_identifiers
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: local environment history

FIELD.LABEL: source rock lithology
FIELD.NAME: source_rock_lithology
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Lithology of source rock (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Source_rock). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: other

FIELD.LABEL: sample material type
FIELD.NAME: sample_material_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The type of material from which the sample was obtained. For the Hydrocarbon package, samples include types like core, rock trimmings, drill cuttings, piping section, coupon, pigging debris, solid deposit, produced fluid, produced water, injected water, swabs, etc. For the Food Package, samples are usually categorized as food, body products or tissues, or environmental material. This field accepts terms listed under environmental specimen (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001246).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: host details

FIELD.LABEL: host of the symbiotic host phenotype
FIELD.NAME: host_of_the_symbiotic_host_phenotype
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Phenotype of the host of the symbiotic host organism. For phenotypic quality ontology (PATO) terms, see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/pato.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: treatment

FIELD.LABEL: biocide administration method
FIELD.NAME: biocide_administration_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method of biocide administration (dose, frequency, duration, time elapsed between last biociding and sampling) (e.g. 150 mg/l; weekly; 4 hr; 3 days)

FIELD.LABEL: chemical treatment
FIELD.NAME: chemical_treatment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: List of chemical compounds administered upstream the sampling location where sampling occurred (e.g. Glycols, H2S scavenger, corrosion and scale inhibitors, demulsifiers, and other production chemicals etc.). The commercial name of the product and name of the supplier should be provided. The date of administration should also be included

FIELD.LABEL: biocide administration
FIELD.NAME: biocide_administration
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: List of biocides (commercial name of product and supplier) and date of administration

FIELD.LABEL: chemical treatment method
FIELD.NAME: chemical_treatment_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method of chemical administration(dose, frequency, duration, time elapsed between administration and sampling) (e.g. 50 mg/l; twice a week; 1 hr; 0 days)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: experimental factor and block

FIELD.LABEL: negative control type
FIELD.NAME: negative_control_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The substance or equipment used as a negative control in an investigation

FIELD.LABEL: positive control type
FIELD.NAME: positive_control_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The substance, mixture, product, or apparatus used to verify that a process which is part of an investigation delivers a true positive.

FIELD.LABEL: experimental factor
FIELD.NAME: experimental_factor
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Experimental factors are essentially the variable aspects of an experiment design which can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI). For a browser of EFO (v 2.95) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/EFO; for a browser of OBI (v 2018-02-12) terms please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/OBI. E.g. time series design [EFO:EFO_0001779]

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics: genetic

FIELD.LABEL: encoded traits
FIELD.NAME: encoded_traits
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Should include key traits like antibiotic resistance or xenobiotic degradation phenotypes for plasmids, converting genes for phage

FIELD.LABEL: subspecific genetic lineage
FIELD.NAME: subspecific_genetic_lineage
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Information about the genetic distinctness of the sequenced organism below the subspecies level, e.g., serovar, serotype, biotype, ecotype, or any relevant genetic typing schemes like Group I plasmid. Subspecies should not be recorded in this term, but in the NCBI taxonomy. Supply both the lineage name and the lineage rank separated by a colon, e.g., biovar:abc123.

FIELD.LABEL: taxonomic classification
FIELD.NAME: taxonomic_classification
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method used for taxonomic classification, along with reference database used, classification rank, and thresholds used to classify new genomes. Expected values are: classification method, database name, and other parameters e.g. vConTACT vContact2 (references from NCBI RefSeq v83, genus rank classification, default parameters)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Reference

FIELD.LABEL: isolation and growth condition
FIELD.NAME: isolation_and_growth_condition
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Publication reference in the form of pubmed ID (pmid), digital object identifier (doi) or url for isolation and growth condition specifications of the organism/material. Mandatory for MIGS and MIMARKS Specimen.

FIELD.LABEL: annotation source
FIELD.NAME: annotation_source
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: For cases where annotation was provided by a community jamboree or model organism database rather than by a specific submitter

FIELD.LABEL: reference for biomaterial
FIELD.NAME: reference_for_biomaterial
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Primary publication if isolated before genome publication; otherwise, primary genome report. Mandatory for MIGS of bacteria and archaea.

FIELD.LABEL: sample subtype
FIELD.NAME: sample_subtype
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of sample sub-type. For example if "sample type" is "Produced Water" then subtype could be "Oil Phase" or "Water Phase". If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample processing

FIELD.LABEL: preservative added to sample
FIELD.NAME: preservative_added_to_sample
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Preservative added to the sample (e.g. Rnalater, alcohol, formaldehyde, etc.). Where appropriate include volume added (e.g. Rnalater; 2 ml)

FIELD.LABEL: single cell or viral particle lysis approach
FIELD.NAME: single_cell_or_viral_particle_lysis_approach
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method used to free DNA from interior of the cell(s) or particle(s)

FIELD.LABEL: single cell or viral particle lysis kit protocol
FIELD.NAME: single_cell_or_viral_particle_lysis_kit_protocol
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the kit or standard protocol used for cell(s) or particle(s) lysis

FIELD.LABEL: sample material processing
FIELD.NAME: sample_material_processing
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed.

FIELD.LABEL: sample volume or weight for DNA extraction
FIELD.NAME: sample_volume_or_weight_for_dna_extraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Volume (ml) or mass (g) of total collected sample processed for DNA extraction. Note: total sample collected should be entered under the term 'sample size'.

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid amplification
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_amplification
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the enzymatic amplification (PCR, TMA, NASBA) of specific nucleic acids

FIELD.LABEL: library size
FIELD.NAME: library_size
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Total number of clones in the library prepared for the project

FIELD.LABEL: library reads sequenced
FIELD.NAME: library_reads_sequenced
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Total number of clones sequenced from the library

FIELD.LABEL: library vector
FIELD.NAME: library_vector
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Cloning vector type(s) used in construction of libraries

FIELD.LABEL: library screening strategy
FIELD.NAME: library_screening_strategy
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Specific enrichment or screening methods applied before and/or after creating clone libraries in order to select a specific group of sequences

FIELD.LABEL: pcr conditions
FIELD.NAME: pcr_conditions
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of reaction conditions and components for PCR in the form of 'initial denaturation:94degC_1.5min; annealing=...'

FIELD.LABEL: pcr primers
FIELD.NAME: pcr_primers
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: PCR primers that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. This field should contain all the primers used for a single PCR reaction if multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction. The primer sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

FIELD.LABEL: adapters
FIELD.NAME: adapters
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Adapters provide priming sequences for both amplification and sequencing of the sample-library fragments. Both adapters should be reported; in uppercase letters

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: food and agriculture

FIELD.LABEL: production start date
FIELD.NAME: production_start_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Date of field's first production

FIELD.LABEL: aromatics wt%
FIELD.NAME: aromatics_wt
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene(SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

FIELD.LABEL: saturates wt%
FIELD.NAME: saturates_wt
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene¬†(SARA) is an analysis method that divides¬†crude oil¬†components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Environmental information

FIELD.LABEL: porosity
FIELD.NAME: porosity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Porosity of deposited sediment is volume of voids divided by the total volume of sample

FIELD.LABEL: formation water salinity
FIELD.NAME: formation_water_salinity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Original formation water salinity (prior to secondary recovery e.g. Waterflooding) expressed as TDS

FIELD.LABEL: basin name
FIELD.NAME: basin_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the basin (e.g. Campos)

FIELD.LABEL: pour point
FIELD.NAME: pour_point
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Temperature at which a liquid becomes semi solid and loses its flow characteristics. In crude oil a high pour point is generally associated with a high paraffin content, typically found in crude deriving from a larger proportion of plant material. (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/pour_point)

FIELD.LABEL: reservoir name
FIELD.NAME: reservoir_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the reservoir (e.g. Carapebus)

FIELD.LABEL: hydrocarbon resource geological age
FIELD.NAME: hydrocarbon_resource_geological_age
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Geological age of hydrocarbon resource (Additional info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Period_(geology)). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: depositional environment
FIELD.NAME: depositional_environment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Main depositional environment (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Depositional_environment). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: source rock kerogen type
FIELD.NAME: source_rock_kerogen_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Origin of kerogen. Type I: Algal (aquatic), Type II: planktonic and soft plant material (aquatic or terrestrial), Type III: terrestrial woody/ fibrous plant material (terrestrial), Type IV: oxidized recycled woody debris (terrestrial) (additional information: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerogen). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: hydrocarbon resource type
FIELD.NAME: hydrocarbon_resource_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Main Hydrocarbon Resource type. The term "Hydrocarbon Resource" HCR defined as a natural environmental feature containing large amounts of hydrocarbons at high concentrations potentially suitable for commercial exploitation. This term should not be confused with the Hydrocarbon Occurrence term which also includes hydrocarbon-rich environments with currently limited commercial interest such as seeps, outcrops, gas hydrates etc. If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: water production rate
FIELD.NAME: water_production_rate
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Water production rates per well (e.g. 987 m3 / day)

FIELD.LABEL: lithology
FIELD.NAME: lithology
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Hydrocarbon resource main lithology (Additional information: http://petrowiki.org/Lithology_and_rock_type_determination). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

FIELD.LABEL: hydrocarbon resource original temperature
FIELD.NAME: hydrocarbon_resource_original_temperature
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Original temperature of the hydrocarbon resource

FIELD.LABEL: depth
FIELD.NAME: depth
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The vertical distance below local surface, e.g. for sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ((0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: local environment conditions imposed

FIELD.LABEL: injection water breakthrough date of specific well
FIELD.NAME: injection_water_breakthrough_date_of_specific_well
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Injection water breakthrough date per well following a secondary and/or tertiary recovery
